volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 1.3.232b - Section 2.2.330b)

sections.label: Section 1.3.232b
sections.original: Dic quod lubet .
sections.translation: Say what you please .

sections.label: Section 1.3.233
sections.original: Non omnino iam perii , est relicuom quo peream magis .
sections.translation: I ’ m not entirely ruined yet ; there ’ s still something more left for me to come to ruin . I have wherewithal to give you what you ask ; but I ’ ll give it you on my own terms ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.235
sections.original: dabo , uti scire possis , perpetuom annum hunc mihi uti serviat
sections.translation: that you may be enabled to understand that throughout all this year she is to be at my service , and that , in the meantime , she is to admit no other man whatever to her , besides myself .

sections.label: Section 1.3.237
sections.original: Quin , si tu voles , domi sérvi qui sunt castrabo viros .
sections.translation: Why , if you choose , the male-servants that are at home , I ’ ll make eunuchs of . In fine , take you care and bring articles of agreement that we will be as you wish . Impose conditions upon us as you wish , and as you shall choose .

sections.label: Section 1.3.240
sections.original: modo tecum una argentum adferto , facile patiar cetera .
sections.translation: Do you only bring the money with you , I ’ ll readily put up with the rest . The doors of procurers are very like those of a custom-house officer ; if you bring anything , then they are opened ; if there is nothing for you to give , then the doors are not opened .

sections.label: Section 1.3.243
sections.original: Interii , si non invenio ego illas viginti minas ,
sections.translation: I ’ m undone , if I don ’ t procure these twenty minae . And really , unless I make away with this much money , I must come to destruction .

sections.label: Section 1.3.245
sections.original: nunc pergam ad forum atque experiar opibus , omni copia ,
sections.translation: Now I ’ ll go to the Forum , and make trial with my resources , with all my endeavours . I ’ ll beg , I ’ ll earnestly entreat each friend as I see him ; both good and bad am I determined to apply to , and make trial of . But if I can ’ t borrow it , I ’ ll take it up at interest , I ’ m resolved .

sections.label: Section 2.1.249
sections.original: Hercle vero , Libane , nunc te meliust expergiscier
sections.translation: Upon my faith , Libanus , it really were better for you now to be waking ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.250
sections.original: atque argento comparando fingere fallaciam .
sections.translation: and to be devising some plan for procuring the money . A long time has now elapsed , since you parted with your master and went to the Forum . For that end that you might devise some plan for procuring the money , there till this time of day have you been sleeping at your ease . Why don ’ t you away with all slothfulness from yourself , and remove all sluggishness ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.255
sections.original: atque ad ingenium vetus versutum te recipis tuom .
sections.translation: and betake yourself again to your former dexterous ingenuity . Preserve your master ; take you care , too , how you do the same that other servants are wont , who employ a clever ingenuity in cheating their master ? Whence shall I get it ? Whom shall I diddle out of it ? Whither shall I steer this fly-boat ? ’ Tis settled , ’ tis confirmed by auspices ; on each side do the birds give good omens .

sections.label: Section 2.1.260
sections.original: picus et cornix ab laeva , corvos parra ab dextera
sections.translation: The woodpecker and the crow are on my right , the raven , as well , upon my left . They are persuading me to it : i ’ faith , I ’ m resolved to follow your advice . ( Starts and listens .) But what means this , that the woodpecker is tapping the elm-tree ? That ’ s not for nothing . Troth , for certain , so far as I can gather omens from augury , the rods are in readiness for my own back , or for Saurea the chamberlain .

sections.label: Section 2.1.265
sections.original: sed quid illúc , quod exanimatus currit huc Leonida ?
sections.translation: But what means this that Leonida is running this way out of breath ? I fear that this bodes ill for my trumped-up schemes

sections.label: Section 2.2.267
sections.original: Vbi ego nunc Libanum requiram aut familiarem filium ,
sections.translation: Where now shall I find Libanus , or my master ’ s son , that I may make them more mirthful than is Mirth herself [*] ? Great booty and a triumph do I bring them on my arrival .

sections.label: Section 2.2.270
sections.original: quando mecum pariter potant , pariter scortari solent ,
sections.translation: Inasmuch as together with me they drink , together with me they are wont to wench , why , this booty that I ’ ve got , together with them will I share it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.272
sections.original: Illic homo aedis compilavit , more si fecit suo .
sections.translation: This fellow has been robbing a house , if he has been acting after his usual manner . Woe to the person that has so carelessly kept the door !

sections.label: Section 2.2.274
sections.original: Aetatem velim servire , Libanum ut conveniam modo .
sections.translation: I could be ready to be a slave for an age , if I could only meet with Libanus .

sections.label: Section 2.2.275
sections.original: Mea quidem hercle ópera liber numquam fies ocius .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , with my assistance , indeed , you shall never be free a bit the sooner .

sections.label: Section 2.2.276
sections.original: Etiam de tergo ducentas plagas praegnatis dabo .
sections.translation: I ’ d give two hundred teeming lashes [*] on my back as well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.277
sections.original: Largitur peculium , omnem in tergo thensaurum gerit .
sections.translation: He ’ s giving away all his substance , for he carries his treasures on his back [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.278
sections.original: Nam si huic sése occasioni tempus supterduxerit ,
sections.translation: But if time should intervene upon this opportunity , never , upon my faith , will he hereafter obtain it again , even with white horses [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.280
sections.original: erum in óbsidione linquet , inimicum animos auxerit .
sections.translation: He ’ ll be deserting his master in the siege ; he ’ ll be increasing the courage of the foe . But if with me he is desirous to seize hold upon this opportunity which has presented itself , very great bounties brimful of joyousness , will he , together with myself , be producing for his masters , both for the son and the father . So that , for life , they will be indebted to us both ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.285
sections.original: nostro devincti beneficio .
sections.translation: bound by our services .

sections.label: Section 2.2.285b
sections.original: Vinctos nescio quos ait ;
sections.translation: He ’ s talking of persons being bound [*] , I don ’ t know who . I don ’ t like it ; I fear for us in common , lest he may have been cheating in some cheatery .

sections.label: Section 2.2.287
sections.original: Perii ego oppido , nisi Libanum invenio iam , ubiubi est gentium .
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone , unless I find Libanus at once , wherever in the world he is .

sections.label: Section 2.2.288
sections.original: Illic homó socium ad malam rem quaerit quem adiungat sibi .
sections.translation: This fellow ’ s looking out for an accomplice , to unite with himself in a bad design . I don ’ t like it : ’ tis a portentous sign that instant , when a person trembles that sweats [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.290
sections.original: Sed quid ego hic properans concesso pedibus , lingua largior ?
sections.translation: But why , as I hasten , do I loiter here with my feet , and make myself so bounteous with my tongue ? Why don ’ t I bid it be quiet , that in its talkativeness is wearing out the day ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.292
sections.original: Edepol hominem ínfelicem , qui patronam comprimat .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , an unfortunate man , to check his patroness ; for if he has done anything roguishly , his tongue perjures itself in his behalf .

sections.label: Section 2.2.294
sections.original: Adproperabo , ne post tempus praedae praesidium parem .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make haste , lest I should be providing a safe keeping for my spoil too late .

sections.label: Section 2.2.295
sections.original: Quae illaec praeda est ? ibo advorsum atque electabo , quidquid est .
sections.translation: What spoil is this ? I ’ ll go meet him , and enquire what it is . ( He accosts him .) I wish you health in as loud a voice as my strength admits of .

sections.label: Section 2.2.297
sections.original: Gymnasium flagri , salveto .
sections.translation: Exerciser of the whip , health to you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.297b
sections.original: Quid agis , custos carceris ?
sections.translation: Keeper of the gaol , how do you do ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.298
sections.original: O catenarum colone .
sections.translation: Ha ! colonizer of the chains .

sections.label: Section 2.2.298b
sections.original: O virgarum lascivia .
sections.translation: Ha ! delight of the scourges .

sections.label: Section 2.2.299
sections.original: Quot pondo ted esse censes nudum ?
sections.translation: When naked , how many pounds do you say you are in weight ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.299b
sections.original: Non edepol scio .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 2.2.300
sections.original: Scibam ego te nescire , at pol ego , qui ted expendi , scio :
sections.translation: I know that you don ’ t know ; but , i ’ faith , I who have weighed you do know . Tied up naked , you were a hundred pounds in weight , when you were hanging with your feet downwards .

sections.label: Section 2.2.302
sections.original: Quo argumento istuc ?
sections.translation: On what evidence is that ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.302b
sections.original: Ego dicam , quo argumento et quo modo .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you on what evidence , and in what way . When you are tied up with a full hundred pounds to your feet [*] , when the manacles are fastened to your hands , and tied to the beam ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.305
sections.original: nec dependes nec propendes — quin malus nequamque sis .
sections.translation: you are weighing neither more nor less , than as being a worthless and good-for-nothing fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.2.306
sections.original: Vae tibi .
sections.translation: Woe be to you !

sections.label: Section 2.2.306b
sections.original: Hoc testamento Servitus legat tibi .
sections.translation: That , Servitude bequeaths to you by her will .

sections.label: Section 2.2.307
sections.original: Verbivelitationem fieri compendi volo .
sections.translation: I wish this skirmishing of words to be cut short . What matter is this ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.308b
sections.original: Certum est credere .
sections.translation: Am I sure in trusting you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.308c
sections.original: Audacter licet .
sections.translation: You may , without hesitation .

sections.label: Section 2.2.309
sections.original: Sis amanti subvenire familiari filio ,
sections.translation: If you wish to assist our master ’ s son in his amour ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.310
sections.original: tantum adest boni improviso , verum commixtum malo :
sections.translation: there is so much of a good opportunity on a sudden , but still mingled with evil — all the hangman ’ s days will be rendered famous by ourselves . Libanus , now have we occasion to find some boldness and inventiveness . An exploit so great have I thought of just now , that we two may be pronounced the most deserving of all for torture to befall us .

sections.label: Section 2.2.315
sections.original: Ergo mirabar quod dudum scapulae gestibant mihi ,
sections.translation: ’ Twas on that account I was wondering why my shoulder-blades were aching just now , which were beginning to prognosticate that there was some danger for them at home . Whatever it is , speak out .

sections.label: Section 2.2.317b
sections.original: Magna est praeda cum magno malo .
sections.translation: ’ Tis great booty with great risk .

sections.label: Section 2.2.318
sections.original: Si quidem omnes coniurati cruciamenta conferant ,
sections.translation: If indeed all persons by compact were to collect all the tortures , I have , I fancy , a back at home , so that I need not seek it out of doors .

sections.label: Section 2.2.320
sections.original: Si istam firmitudinem animi óptines , salvi sumus .
sections.translation: If you maintain such firmness of resolve , then we are all right .

sections.label: Section 2.2.321
sections.original: Quin si tergo res solvenda est , rapere cupio publicum :
sections.translation: Why , if the matter were to be atoned for by my back , I could wish to seize the public money : I ’ ll persist in my denial , and I ’ ll endure all ; in fine , I ’ ll forswear myself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.323
sections.original: Em ista virtus est , quando usust qui malum fert fortiter ;
sections.translation: Ah ! that ’ s true valour , when occasion is , for one to endure misfortune with boldness . He that endures misfortune with boldness , that man afterwards enjoys good fortune .

sections.label: Section 2.2.325
sections.original: Quin rem actutum edisseris ? cupio malum nanciscier .
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you tell the matter at once ? I ’ m longing to tempt the scourge .

sections.label: Section 2.2.326
sections.original: Placide ergo unum quidquid rogita , ut adquiescam . non vides
sections.translation: Ask deliberately each particular then , that I may rest me . Don ’ t you see that I ’ m still out of breath with running ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.327b
sections.original: Age , age , mansero
sections.translation: Well , well , I ’ ll wait your pleasure , even , in fact , till you die .

sections.label: Section 2.2.328b
sections.original: Vbinam est erus ?
sections.translation: Where ’ s our master , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.329
sections.original: Maior apud forumst , minor hic est intus .
sections.translation: The old one is at the Forum , the young one is here in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.2.329b
sections.original: Iam satis est mihi .
sections.translation: That ’ s enough for me then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.330
sections.original: Túm igitur tu dives es factus ?
sections.translation: Is it then that you ’ ve become a rich man ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.330b
sections.original: Mitte ridicularia .
sections.translation: Leave off your raillery .
